title: Matelea purpurea
intro: Matelea purpurea, also known as Purple Swallow Wort or Purple Milk Vine, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including anxiety, insomnia, and inflammation, due to its adaptogenic and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Matelea purpurea include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Matelea purpurea include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and improve sleep quality.
taxonomy: Matelea purpurea is classified within the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This plant is further categorized as a species within the genus Matelea. The genus Matelea is a group of flowering plants native to the Americas, with species found in various regions including Central and South America, as well as the southern United States. Within the Apocynaceae family, Matelea purpurea is part of the tribe Plumerieae. The classification of Matelea purpurea highlights its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships within the plant kingdom.
